description: Track code churn rate — percentage of own lines reworked within N days

userStories.title: DB migration: commit_line_stats table
userStories.description: Add a new migration in `db.rs` `open_db()` that creates `commit_line_stats`. This table stores per-commit line-level stats (lines added, deleted, net) for all files touched by each own commit. Keyed by repo_path + commit_hash. Used later for churn detection.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration appended to the Migrations array in open_db()
Table: commit_line_stats(id, repo_path TEXT, commit_hash TEXT, file_path TEXT, lines_added INTEGER, lines_deleted INTEGER, committed_at TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')))
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX on (repo_path, commit_hash, file_path)
open_db() runs migrations cleanly on a fresh DB (cargo test passes)
open_db() runs migrations cleanly on a DB from migration N-1 (idempotent)

userStories.title: DB migration: churn_events table
userStories.description: Add another migration that creates `churn_events`. Each row records that a specific file first written in commit A was subsequently modified in commit B within the churn window. This is the raw evidence for churn rate computation.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration appended after US-001 migration
Table: churn_events(id, repo_path TEXT, original_commit_hash TEXT, churn_commit_hash TEXT, file_path TEXT, lines_churned INTEGER, churn_window_days INTEGER, detected_at TEXT NOT NULL)
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX on (repo_path, original_commit_hash, churn_commit_hash, file_path)
open_db() runs all migrations cleanly

Uses INSERT OR IGNORE; returns true if row inserted, false if duplicate

CommitLineStat struct: repo_path, commit_hash, file_path, lines_added, lines_deleted, committed_at (DateTime<Utc>)
Unit test: insert two stats for same commit+file → second is ignored
Unit test: query returns rows in committed_at ASC order

userStories.title: insert helper for churn_events
userStories.description: Add `insert_churn_event` to `db.rs`. Uses INSERT OR IGNORE. No query helper needed yet — churn rate is computed in-memory from commit_line_stats.

userStories.title: git diff analysis: extract per-file line stats from a commit
userStories.description: Add `diff_commit_stats(repo: &Repository, commit: &Commit) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<FileLineStat>>` in a new `src/churn.rs` module. Uses git2 diff to walk parent→commit and collect lines_added/lines_deleted per file. Skips binary files, merge commits (parent_count > 1), and renames (treat rename as deletion of old + addition of new path).
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/churn.rs with pub fn diff_commit_stats
Returns Vec<FileLineStat> where FileLineStat { file_path: String, lines_added: u32, lines_deleted: u32 }
Merge commits (parent_count > 1): return empty Vec (skip)
Binary files: skip (git2 Delta::Binary or hunk callback not called)
Renamed files: emit two entries — old path with lines_deleted = old line count, new path with lines_added = new line count
Repo with no parent (initial commit): diff against empty tree
Unit test: single-file commit adding 5 lines → FileLineStat { lines_added: 5, lines_deleted: 0 }
Unit test: commit editing existing file (3 added, 2 deleted) → correct counts
Unit test: merge commit → empty result
Unit test: initial commit (no parent) → counts all added lines

userStories.title: Backfill commit_line_stats during daemon poll
userStories.description: In `git_ops.rs`, after inserting a commit into `git_activity`, call `churn::diff_commit_stats` and insert results into `commit_line_stats` via `db::insert_commit_line_stats`. Apply both on backfill (first-poll path) and on incremental new commits. Only runs for own commits (already filtered by is_own_commit).
acceptanceCriteria: poll_repo calls diff_commit_stats for every commit it would record
Inserts one commit_line_stats row per file per commit
Skips merge commits (diff_commit_stats returns empty for them)
committed_at stored as RFC3339 string from commit.time()
No crash if diff fails (log warn, continue)
Integration test: poll a test repo with two commits → commit_line_stats has correct row count
Integration test: re-poll same commits → no duplicate rows (INSERT OR IGNORE)

userStories.title: Churn detection algorithm
userStories.description: Add `compute_churn(conn: &Connection, repo_path: &str, window_days: u32) -> anyhow::Result<ChurnReport>` in `src/churn.rs`. Algorithm: (1) fetch all commit_line_stats for repo within the last window_days. (2) Group stats by file_path. (3) For each file, sort commits by committed_at. (4) For each pair (commit_A, commit_B) where commit_B > commit_A and both touch the same file, if commit_B.committed_at - commit_A.committed_at <= window_days: lines in commit_B that overlap commit_A's lines are churned. Approximation: min(commit_A.lines_added, commit_B.lines_deleted) = churned_lines. (5) Sum all lines_added across all commits = total_lines_written. (6) churn_rate = churned_lines / total_lines_written * 100.0.
acceptanceCriteria: ChurnReport struct: { repo_path: String, window_days: u32, total_lines_written: u64, churned_lines: u64, churn_rate_pct: f64, commit_count: usize, churn_event_count: usize }
compute_churn returns ChurnReport
churn_rate_pct = 0.0 if total_lines_written == 0
Only considers own commits (already filtered at insert time in US-006)
Inserts detected churn events into churn_events table via insert_churn_event
Unit test: two commits to same file within window → non-zero churn
Unit test: two commits to same file outside window → zero churn
Unit test: two commits to different files → zero churn
Unit test: total_lines_written = 0 → churn_rate_pct = 0.0 (no divide-by-zero)

impl Default for churn_window_days returns 14
Existing configs without the field deserialize without error (serde default)
Unit test: Config::default() gives churn_window_days = 14
Existing config-related tests still pass

userStories.title: CLI command: blackbox churn
userStories.description: Add `Churn` variant to `Commands` enum in `cli.rs` and a match arm in `main.rs`. Accepts optional `--window <days>` (overrides config), `--repo <path>` (filter to one repo), `--format pretty|json|csv`. Calls compute_churn for each tracked repo (or filtered repo) and renders output.

Match arm in main.rs dispatches to run_churn()
run_churn() loads config, opens DB, determines window (--window > config.churn_window_days), discovers repo list from git_activity (distinct repo_paths), calls compute_churn per repo
If --repo provided, filters to that repo_path only
If no repos have any commit_line_stats, prints 'No churn data — run blackbox start to collect data.'
cargo build passes
Integration test via assert_cmd: blackbox churn --help exits 0 and contains 'churn'

userStories.description: Implement render_churn_pretty(reports: &[ChurnReport]) -> String in src/output.rs. Shows a table: repo name, window, lines written, lines churned, churn %. Color-codes churn rate: green < 10%, yellow 10-25%, red > 25%. Shows global aggregate at bottom.

Prints header: '=== Code Churn (last N days) ==='
Per-repo row: repo_name | lines_written | churned | churn_rate_pct%
Churn rate colored: green if < 10.0, yellow if 10.0..=25.0, red if > 25.0
Global summary line: 'Total: X lines written, Y churned (Z%)'
Empty reports: prints 'No churn data yet.'

userStories.title: Integration test: end-to-end churn detection
userStories.description: Add tests/churn_test.rs. Build a temp git repo with at least 3 commits: commit A adds 10 lines to file.txt, commit B (within window) modifies 4 lines of file.txt, commit C adds a new file new.txt with 5 lines. Call poll_repo then compute_churn. Assert churn_rate_pct is approximately 4/15 * 100 (≈26.7%). Also test window boundary: if B is older than window_days, churn = 0.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/churn_test.rs created
Test: three-commit repo → churn_rate_pct ≈ 26.7% (within 1.0 tolerance)
Test: same repo with window=0 days → churn_rate_pct = 0.0 (B outside window)
Test: repo with only one commit → churn_rate_pct = 0.0
Test: repo with no commit_line_stats → ChurnReport has total_lines_written=0 and churn_rate_pct=0.0
All tests pass with cargo test
